task_metadata.current_task: Implement on(eventName, listener) method to register event listeners
task_metadata.status: Implementing listener registration with method chaining support

nodes.description: Create EventEmitter class with on, emit, and off methods to allow event-driven communication
nodes.rationale: The task requires implementing a standard event emitter pattern. A class-based approach provides an encapsulated, reusable solution with clear method APIs for registering listeners, triggering events, and removing listeners.
nodes.task_state: Starting implementation of EventEmitter class with core methods

nodes.description: Event listeners must be stored in a data structure that allows efficient addition, removal, and iteration
nodes.rationale: The off method requires O(n) removal capability, while on and emit need efficient storage access. Using a Map or array of listeners per event name provides the necessary performance characteristics for these operations.

nodes.description: Use a Map data structure with event names as keys and arrays of listener functions as values
nodes.rationale: A Map provides O(1) lookup by event name, while arrays allow efficient push/pop operations for adding listeners and linear search for removal. This is a standard approach balancing simplicity and performance.
nodes.task_state: Designing listener storage mechanism using Map<string, Function[]>

nodes.description: Implement on(eventName, listener) method to register event listeners
nodes.rationale: The on method is a core requirement. It should accept an event name string and a callback function, storing the listener for later invocation. It should return 'this' to support method chaining.
nodes.task_state: Implementing listener registration with method chaining support

edges.rationale: The EventEmitter design is constrained by the need for efficient listener storage and removal, which influences the choice of data structure for maintaining the listener registry.
The on method implements the listener registration pattern using the Map data structure previously designed for storing listeners by event name.
